key_insight: Compositionality in learned representations emerges only in a narrow sweet spot of network depth and connectivity, and otherwise gradient descent tends to converge to fractured solutions.
engineering_relevance: For model design in computational science and engineering, this suggests that sparsity and depth must be tuned structurally—not just made smaller—to recover reusable, generalizable primitives.
method: Similarity-based pruning (SP) to recover compositional connectivity
Heuristic depth predictor to estimate where compositionality is most likely to appear
Theoretical analysis using compositional sparsity, volume-ratio arguments, and feature-interference bounds
evidence_signal: The abstract reports empirical evaluation across different depths and sparsity/connectivity patterns, showing compositionality peaks only in specific target-dependent regimes.
watch_item: Compositionality depends on which connections remain, so naive pruning or depth changes may fail even if overall sparsity seems appropriate.
paper_title: Compositionality Emerges in a Narrow Depth-Connectivity Regime: Architecture Constraints and Solution Manifolds
